column1:
The proposed architecture looks good and I want to proceed with it.

Constraints to follow:
- Backend: Node.js + Express + TypeScript
- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript
- Tests: Cucumber with Gherkin in /testes
- Persistence: JSON files only (no database)
- Email: SMTP configurable via .env, with a development fallback (e.g., Ethereal)
- Email rule: send at most 1 email per day per student, consolidating all changes

Now, define the initial folder and file structure for the project.

Keep it:
- simple
- well-organized
- easy to scale

Do NOT implement full features yet — just structure, basic setup, and responsibilities of each part.
column2:
ele tinha um background do outro experimento e com um unico prompt foi realizando tudo.

column1:
Now I want to define the data models before implementing anything.

Entities:
- Student (name, CPF, email)
- Class (description, year, semester)
- Evaluation (student × class × meta with values MANA, MPA, MA)
- EmailLog (to support daily email consolidation)

Please:
1. Propose TypeScript interfaces/types for all entities
2. Suggest how to model:
   - students inside a class
   - evaluations per class and per student
   - metas (e.g., Requisitos, Testes, etc.)
3. Define the JSON structure for:
   - students.json
   - classes.json
   - evaluations.json
   - emailLog.json

Focus on clarity, consistency, and avoiding future complexity.

Do NOT implement routes yet — only design the data structure.
column3: Os modelos já estão definidos no types/index.ts criado na estrutura inicial.
column4: retornou as etapas já criadas dessa parte

column1:
Now implement the Students CRUD in the backend.

Requirements:
- Routes:
  - GET /students
  - POST /students
  - PUT /students/:id
  - DELETE /students/:id
- Persist data in data/students.json
- Validate:
  - required fields (name, CPF, email)
  - basic format (email)
- Handle errors properly
- Keep code modular (routes, services, utils if needed)

Do NOT implement classes or evaluations yet.

Explain:
- which files you created
- responsibilities of each file
- how to test the endpoints

column1:
Now implement the Evaluations feature.

Requirements:
- Each class should display:
  - a table
  - rows = students
  - columns = metas (e.g., Requisitos, Testes, etc.)
- Each cell contains a concept:
  - MANA, MPA, or MA
- Allow editing concepts per student per meta
- Persist evaluations in evaluations.json

Important:
- Follow the data model previously defined
- Avoid duplication or inconsistent structures

Explain how evaluations are stored and updated.

column5:
The Evaluations page is live. Here's what was implemented:

Class dropdown — select a turma to load its evaluations
Student × Goal table — rows = enrolled students, columns = Requisitos / Testes / Implementação / Documentação
Inline <select> per cell — options: — (blank), MANA, MPA, MA; fires PUT /classes/:id/evaluations on change
Color coding — MA = green, MPA = yellow, MANA = red, empty = white
Per-cell spinner while saving, error banner at top
All 14 Cucumber scenarios still pass, TypeScript compiles clean

column1:
Now implement the email notification logic.

Rules:
- When an evaluation is created or updated, register the change
- Each student must receive at most 1 email per day
- The email must consolidate all evaluation changes from that day
- A student may be enrolled in multiple classes, and all changes must be included

Implementation details:
- Use emailLog.json to track changes
- You can simulate email sending (console log or mock service)

Focus on correctness of the consolidation logic, not on real email delivery.

Explain how the daily grouping works.

column3:
Now I have the complete picture. Here are the issues I found:

Backend:

db.ts — JSON.parse without try-catch; corrupt file crashes the server
routes/*.ts — brittle msg.includes('não encontrad[o|a]') for 404/400 detection
classRoutes.ts DELETE — evaluationService.deleteByClass runs before existence check
classService.addStudent — doesn't verify the student ID exists (dangling reference)
evaluationService.upsert — no runtime validation of goal/value against allowed enums
Frontend:
6. API constant duplicated across three page files
7. Navigation.tsx — all styles inline, unreadable
8. Students.css, Classes.css, Evaluations.css — ~80 lines of button/alert/form/badge CSS copied identically across all three files

Let me implement all fixes now:
